research_topics: Adaptive Bollinger Band / Keltner Channel strategies
Equity curve trading / drawdown circuit breakers

findings.finding: A 2025 framework combines Hidden Markov Models with ensemble tree models (bagging + boosting) for regime detection, then trains regime-specific ML models. The HMM detects 3 states (bull/bear/neutral) using features: returns, volatility, VIX, MA50-MA200 spread, 10Y yield. A hybrid voting classifier integrates HMM with ensemble learners for robust regime classification. Python package 'hidden-regime' on PyPI provides pipeline-based architecture with financial pipelines, regime characterization, and trading signals.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our current regime detection uses a simple EMA9/EMA21 spread + ATR threshold (4 states). Upgrading to HMM-based detection with macro features would better capture regime transitions. The 'hidden-regime' PyPI package could be integrated directly. Our REGIME_WEIGHTS dict already has per-regime multipliers -- HMM would improve WHEN those multipliers activate.

findings.topic: RAM-Net: Meta-Learning for Regime-Aware Sample Weighting
findings.finding: RAM-Net (ACM ICAIF 2025) dynamically assigns sample weights conditioned on both loss patterns and current market regime. Instead of static regime multipliers, it uses a meta-learning architecture that reinterprets historical data according to relevance under prevailing market conditions. Enables upweighting training samples that resemble current conditions.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our signal accuracy is computed with a fixed 70/30 recent/all-time blend (_RECENCY_WEIGHT_NORMAL = 0.7). RAM-Net's approach suggests we should weight historical signal accuracy by regime similarity, not just recency. A signal that worked well in the LAST ranging period is more relevant than one that worked last week in a trending period.

findings.topic: Hybrid AI Trading with Regime Gating and Composite Scoring
findings.finding: A 2026 paper (ComSIA/Springer LNNS) combines ML predictions, technical confirmation (price > EMA-50 + bullish MACD), and mean-reversion (RSI < 30) into a composite score requiring >= 2 for entry. Regime acts as gate: trades only in bullish regimes. Results: 135.49% return vs 8.64% baseline, Sharpe 1.68 vs 0.48, max drawdown -15.6% vs -19.84%. Position sizing via ATR: 0.01 * cash / ATR.
findings.relevance_to_us: We already have composite scoring and regime gating (REGIME_GATED_SIGNALS). The key insight is their composite score threshold requiring agreement from DIFFERENT signal categories (ML + technical + MR). Our MIN_VOTERS=3 is similar but doesn't enforce category diversity. Adding a 'must have votes from >= 2 signal categories' rule could reduce false consensus from correlated signals.

findings.finding: A Python WFO implementation uses Bayesian Optimization for parameter tuning within rolling in-sample windows, validated on out-of-sample. Combining LLMs for hypothesis generation, genetic programming for parameter optimization, and neural networks for regime detection -- all validated through WFO -- yields honest Sharpe of 0.33. XGBoost + walk-forward specifically addresses concept drift.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our REGIME_WEIGHTS, ACCURACY_GATE_THRESHOLD, _CORRELATION_PENALTY, and _RECENCY_WEIGHT values are all manually tuned. Walk-forward optimization would automatically re-tune these on a rolling basis (e.g., every 30 days on last 90 days, validate on next 30). This directly addresses 11/30 signals having <45% recent accuracy -- they may have been tuned for a different regime.

findings.topic: Signal Correlation Pruning via PCA and Correlation Filtering
findings.finding: Best practice: (1) Compute pairwise correlation matrix of signal outputs over rolling window, (2) Apply PCA to identify redundant signals, (3) Within each cluster keep only highest-accuracy signal, (4) LASSO regularization naturally zeros out redundant signals. Practical approach: correlation check to remove similar signals, take top uncorrelated performers into ensemble.
findings.relevance_to_us: We have 5 manual CORRELATION_GROUPS. An automated rolling 30-day pairwise Pearson correlation (signal outputs as BUY=1/HOLD=0/SELL=-1) would discover NEW correlations and dissolve stale ones. Current groups may miss undiscovered correlations (momentum_factors + oscillators? fibonacci + mean_reversion?).

findings.topic: Mean-Reversion in Ranging Markets: Z-Score + OU Half-Life Monitoring
findings.finding: Mean reversion has 65-75% win rate in ranging markets (matching our observed 65.4%). Best indicators: Z-score (more precise than RSI), RSI(2) for ultra-short-term, IBS for intraday, OU half-life for persistence. A 2025 SSRN paper refines half-life estimation. Key risk: MR fails catastrophically on regime shift. Forex spends 70-80% of time in consolidation. Dynamic OU half-life monitoring detects when MR is breaking down (half-life increasing = regime shifting).
findings.relevance_to_us: Our mean_reversion already has RSI(2), RSI(3), IBS, BB %B, and OU half-life. What's MISSING: (1) Z-score MR signal (more granular than RSI), (2) Regime-conditional stop-losses for MR trades, (3) Dynamic OU half-life monitoring to detect regime shift early -- when half-life increases 2x above 30-day average, emit warning.

findings.finding: When Bollinger Bands contract INSIDE Keltner Channels, it signals extreme low volatility (squeeze). This compression acts as a coiled spring. In range-bound markets, outer KC bands act as dynamic S/R for MR trades. KC mean-reversion on S&P 500: 77% win rate, 288 trades, CAGR 6.3%, profit factor 2.0. Combining RSI + volume alongside squeeze validates breakout direction.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our volatility signal has BB Squeeze detection but no Keltner Channel overlay. Adding KC for squeeze-within-squeeze pattern would improve precision. In ranging regime, BB/KC squeeze could serve as TIMING signal -- MR trades during squeeze, breakout trades on squeeze release.

findings.finding: Kelly-VAPS bridges Kelly Criterion with ATR-based volatility adjustment. Fractional Kelly (0.25-0.5x) reduces volatility while maintaining growth. A 2025 gold futures paper achieved Sharpe 2.88 using fractional impact-adjusted Kelly with ATR exits. Formula: position_size = 0.25 * kelly_fraction * equity / (ATR * tick_value). For crypto (>30% vol), use 0.25x Kelly.
findings.relevance_to_us: Current position sizing is confidence-based. Kelly-VAPS would make sizing volatility-aware: smaller in high-ATR environments (BTC at $67K with high vol), larger in low-vol (stable XAU). MR trades should be sized larger (high win rate) but with tight stops.

findings.finding: Stop-losses IMPROVE risk-adjusted returns in trending but REDUCE returns in mean-reverting regimes. Trailing stops with dynamic thresholds outperform fixed (Sharpe 1.28 vs 0.92 on commodities). Recommended: calm markets 1.5-2x ATR, volatile 2.5-3x ATR. In MR regimes, wider stop-loss policies outperform only when bull-regime volatility is low.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our risk_management.py uses ATR-based stops without regime adjustment. In current ranging regime, stops may trigger on normal oscillation. Widening to 2.5-3x ATR in ranging and tightening to 1.5x in trending would reduce false stop-outs. For MR trades, stops should be beyond range boundary (Donchian 55) not ATR-based.

findings.topic: Equity Curve Trading: Serial Correlation Detection
findings.finding: Equity curve strategies reduce drawdown ONLY where returns show serial correlation (losses beget losses). Implementation: track equity curve MA, reduce position sizes by 25-30% when equity drops below MA, set alerts at 75% of drawdown limit. Anti-pattern: ECT is HARMFUL when returns are mean-reverting (which is common in MR strategies).
findings.relevance_to_us: Our check_drawdown() has binary 20% threshold. Adding graduated scale (75% of limit = reduce 25%; 90% = reduce 50%) would be more nuanced. Should test whether our equity curve shows serial correlation before applying ECT.

findings.topic: TradingAgents: Multi-Agent LLM Framework with Bull/Bear Debate
findings.finding: TradingAgents v0.2.0 (Feb 2026) deploys 7 specialized agents: 4 analysts, bull/bear researchers who debate in natural language, a trader, and 3-perspective risk management team. Built on LangGraph. Results: AAPL 26.62% return, Sharpe 8.21, max drawdown 0.91%. Architecture: structured outputs for control + natural language for debate. Agents query global state directly. Key benefit: debate format surfaces risks single-agent misses.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our Layer 2 is a single Claude instance. TradingAgents pattern suggests: (1) Bull researcher argues FOR trade, (2) Bear argues AGAINST, (3) Risk manager evaluates. Could implement as 3 sequential claude -p calls with specialized prompts where each sees previous arguments. Would reduce Layer 2's tendency toward either excessive caution or aggression.

findings.topic: Adaptive Regime Boundary via RL Controller
findings.finding: March 2026 paper proposes autoencoder-gated dual transformers with Soft Actor-Critic RL to continuously optimize the BOUNDARY between regimes, not just signal weights within each. Achieves 0.59% MAPE. Key: when volatility is low, a 0.3% spread might be 'trending', but when vol is high, 0.5% is noise. The boundary itself must be adaptive.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our regime boundary is a fixed 0.5% EMA spread threshold. ALL instruments currently classified as 'ranging' -- possibly because threshold is too aggressive. A rolling-percentile approach to regime thresholds (e.g., 0.5% = 60th percentile of recent spreads means 'ranging', not a fixed cutoff) would reduce misclassification.

findings.topic: Dual MR + Trend Portfolio with Rebalancing Bonus
findings.finding: MR has 65-75% win rate with capped payoff (concave, short vol); trend following has 10-40% win rate with massive payoff (convex, long vol). 50/50 monthly rebalanced portfolio captures 'rebalancing bonus' via volatility harvesting. MR thrives in current choppy conditions; trend following gets whipped.
findings.relevance_to_us: Currently one consensus per ticker with MR + trend signals all voting together. Should compute TWO consensus scores: MR-consensus and TREND-consensus. In ranging regime, only MR-consensus drives decisions. In trending, only TREND-consensus. Eliminates trend signals (40-44% accuracy) diluting MR signals (65-68%) in ranging markets.

recommended_improvements.title: Dual-Consensus Architecture: Separate MR and Trend Voting Pools
recommended_improvements.description: Split the 32-signal voting into two parallel consensus pools: MR-pool (mean_reversion, fibonacci, bb, rsi, calendar, econ_calendar) and TREND-pool (ema, macd, trend, momentum_factors, heikin_ashi, structure, smart_money). In ranging regime, only MR-pool votes count. In trending, only TREND-pool. In high-vol, both with equal weight. This eliminates trend signals (40-44% accuracy in ranging) diluting high-performing MR signals (65-68%). The regime selector already exists; this separates voting downstream. Neutral signals (volume, sentiment, ministral, qwen3, claude_fundamental) vote in both pools.

recommended_improvements.title: Regime-Adaptive Stop-Loss Multipliers
recommended_improvements.description: Add regime-based ATR multiplier to risk_management.py: ranging=2.5x ATR (wider to survive oscillation), trending=1.5x ATR (tighter to protect profits), high-vol=3.0x ATR (widest to avoid noise). For MR trades, set stops beyond Donchian 55 high/low rather than ATR-based. Research shows stop-losses reduce returns in MR regimes with fixed thresholds but improve with dynamic ones. Smallest change with highest ROI.

recommended_improvements.title: Automated Rolling Correlation Matrix for Signal Pruning
recommended_improvements.description: Compute 30-day rolling pairwise Pearson correlation of signal outputs (BUY=1, HOLD=0, SELL=-1) per ticker. Auto-discover correlation groups when r > 0.6. Replace manually-maintained CORRELATION_GROUPS with dynamically-computed groups. Log discovered correlations to JSONL. Only highest-accuracy signal per auto-detected group gets full weight. Run as part of daily accuracy update cycle.

recommended_improvements.title: Walk-Forward Optimization for Regime Weights
recommended_improvements.description: Monthly WFO re-optimizing REGIME_WEIGHTS, ACCURACY_GATE_THRESHOLD, _CORRELATION_PENALTY using last 90 days of signal_log as in-sample and next 30 days as out-of-sample. Use optuna (Bayesian optimization) to maximize accuracy. Only adopt new weights if they improve out-of-sample. Run as scheduled task (PF-WFO, monthly). Addresses root problem: manually-set regime weights may be stale.

recommended_improvements.title: HMM-Based Regime Detection Upgrade
recommended_improvements.description: Replace EMA9/EMA21 spread + ATR threshold with Gaussian HMM (hmmlearn) trained on: 20-day returns, 20-day volatility, VIX, EMA spread, ADX. 1 year training data, 3-4 states mapped to existing regime labels. Retrain monthly. May resolve why ALL instruments are classified 'ranging' -- fixed 0.5% threshold may be wrong for current market volatility.

recommended_improvements.title: Kelly-VAPS Position Sizing
recommended_improvements.description: Replace confidence-based sizing with fractional Kelly (0.25x) adjusted by ATR: position_size = 0.25 * kelly_fraction * equity / (ATR_14 * fx_rate). Kelly fraction from per-ticker accuracy data. Naturally sizes larger for high-accuracy tickers (XAG 64.8%) and smaller for low (ETH 46%). Cap 5% per position.

recommended_improvements.title: Z-Score MR Sub-Signal + OU Half-Life Monitor
recommended_improvements.description: Add z-score sub-indicator to mean_reversion.py: z = (price - SMA_20) / std_20. BUY at z < -2.0, SELL at z > 2.0. Add dynamic OU half-life monitoring: when half-life > 2x its 30-day average, emit regime-shift warning. More granular than RSI for MR timing.

recommended_improvements.title: Keltner Channel Overlay for Enhanced Squeeze Detection
recommended_improvements.description: Add KC (EMA-20, 1.5x ATR) to volatility.py. Detect BB-inside-KC squeeze. In ranging, use KC as dynamic MR targets. On squeeze release, switch to breakout mode. Enhances existing BB Squeeze with second volatility envelope.

recommended_improvements.title: Graduated Drawdown Circuit Breaker
recommended_improvements.description: Replace binary 20% threshold with graduated response: -10% = reduce sizes 25%, -15% = reduce 50%, -18% = reduce 75%, -20% = full halt. Test returns for serial correlation before applying equity curve trading. MR strategies often have mean-reverting returns, making ECT harmful.

recommended_improvements.title: Multi-Agent Debate Layer 2 Architecture
recommended_improvements.description: Restructure Layer 2 from single Claude invocation to 3-stage debate: (1) Bull Agent argues FOR trade, (2) Bear Agent sees Bull's argument and argues AGAINST, (3) Decision Agent sees both + risk metrics and decides. Each stage is separate claude -p call with specialized prompts. Mirrors TradingAgents' proven approach (Sharpe 8.21). Debate format naturally surfaces risks single-agent misses.
